title: Document Layout Analysis & Table Extraction
concept: Document layout analysis identifies structural elements like columns, headers, tables, and reading order in document scans.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the primary benefit of attention-based OCR over sequential OCR?

options.text: It uses global language context to resolve ambiguous and messy handwriting.
It does not require GPUs to run.
It is faster for single characters.

exercises.explain: Correct! Transformers utilize self-attention to parse surrounding letters, greatly improving transcription accuracy on messy handwriting.

cards.title: Reading in order
cards.body: If you open a newspaper, the text is split into vertical columns. If a simple OCR model read the page from left to right, line by line, it would read the first line of Column 1, jump to the first line of Column 2, and create a scrambled, unreadable paragraph. We must understand the page layout first.

cards.title: Layout Analysis & Table Extraction
cards.body: Document Layout Analysis (DLA) is the process of identifying the spatial structure of a document. It classifies regions into Headers, Paragraphs, Images, and Tables. Table Extraction is a specialized sub-task that parses rows and columns to output structured data (like CSVs).

cards.title: A town planning map
cards.body: DLA is like zoning a town map. You label the park (images), the commercial strip (text columns), and the library database (tables). Without zoning, you would build roads straight through houses. DLA ensures the OCR scanner reads columns in the correct sequence.

cards.title: Parsing balance sheets
cards.body: Financial institutions process scanned PDF quarterly reports. They use layout models (like LayoutLM) to locate table boundaries, extract cell coordinates, and convert the scanned numbers into structured Excel tables automatically.

cards.title: Table lines are not required
cards.body: You might think table extraction only works if the tables have clear black grid lines. Modern layout models can detect tables that have no borders (borderless tables) by analyzing alignment, empty spacing, and column patterns.

cards.title: Layout Parsing
cards.body: How layout analysis guides OCR reading order:

left.text:
Reads full screen left-to-right
Merges adjacent columns
Outputs scrambled sentences

right.text:
Segments columns first
Follows intended reading order
Preserves document integrity

exercises.prompt: What is the primary purpose of Document Layout Analysis?

options.text: To identify and classify structural regions like columns, headers, and tables on a page
To correct the grammar of the transcribed text
To compress the image size to make it load faster
To detect faces in the document photos

exercises.explain: Layout analysis identifies document components and their boundaries to preserve visual structures.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Table extraction models can only detect tables that have printed borders and solid black lines.
exercises.explain: False. Modern layout networks can extract borderless tables by analyzing text alignment and spatial spacing.

exercises.sentence: If you read multi-column documents horizontally, the OCR will output ___ text.
exercises.explain: Reading columns horizontally blends different sentences together, rendering the text unintelligible.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is a task handled by layout analysis models?

options.text: Labeling a region as a multi-column article and determining the correct reading order
Matching a signature to verify user identity
Filtering noise from an audio clip

exercises.explain: Segmenting document structures and establishing reading paths is the core objective of layout analysis.

exercises.prompt: You are building an archive scanner to digitize old historical newspapers. The pages contain 5 vertical columns, mixed with images and large advertisements. How should you design the ingestion pipeline?

options.text: Run a layout analysis model to segment the page into columns and articles, determine the correct reading blocks, and send each block in order to the OCR engine.
Pass the full newspaper image directly to a sequential OCR model, scanning from top to bottom.
Only run table extraction, since newspapers are formatted in tables.
Use a style transfer model to convert the newspaper to a modern font first.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Column-based layouts must be mapped by a layout analysis model first to establish the correct reading blocks. Passing the entire page blindly results in scrambled columns. Outstanding work! You have finished the Optical Character Recognition unit. Next up: Image Captioning Architectures.
